select_when: The work requires diagnosing whether someone is stuck at recall, understanding, application, analysis, evaluation, or synthesis rather than treating learning as one vague block.
Skill-building needs a progression from comprehension to execution to judgment instead of a jump straight to critique.
Curriculum, coaching, or onboarding must sequence challenge so deeper performance rests on real foundations.
A learner or team needs a structured roadmap for what kind of thinking comes next.
avoid_when: The taxonomy is being treated as a rigid staircase that blocks iteration, creativity, or revisiting earlier levels when real learning loops back.
People are trying to evaluate or critique before they have actually analyzed the problem or built enough understanding to support judgment.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on stage diagnosis, sequencing, and anti-premature-critique discipline rather than on broad education-theory commentary.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor curriculum-taxonomy sprawl because the raw source is already strongest on diagnosing the next cognitive move.
open_questions: The source reaches into strategy and creativity, but the operational file stays on stage diagnosis and progression quality.

select_when.note: The raw source explicitly says the model is strongest when diagnosing whether someone is stuck at recall, understanding, application, or synthesis and when challenge must be sequenced.

avoid_when.note: The source explicitly warns against rigid staircase use and against evaluation without enough analytical foundation.

input_type.note: The source is explicit about stage confusion and progression gaps; the compact input label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The raw model yields a sequenced cognitive progression; the short output label compresses that into an operational type.

reasoning_types.note: The model diagnoses learning-stage position and next-step reflection, but the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
